race.name: Kungsledenrännet
race.tagline: Eighty kilometers through the Vindelfjällen wilderness — Sweden's most remote ski race crosses a nature reserve where reindeer outnumber spectators.

climate.description: April above the Arctic Circle still delivers full winter conditions at altitude. The Vindelfjällen mountains ensure cold temperatures and deep snow well into spring. Typically -10°C to 0°C. Weather can change rapidly in the exposed mountain terrain, with wind being the primary hazard.
challenges: Exposed mountain terrain with rapid weather changes
High winds on open fjäll sections
Remote wilderness with limited rescue access
Whiteout conditions possible in mountain passes
Self-sufficiency required across the long course

course.primary: An 80km wilderness race through the Vindelfjällen Nature Reserve, one of Europe's largest protected mountain areas. The course follows ancient Sami reindeer migration routes across exposed mountain plateaus, through sheltered valleys, and past frozen mountain lakes. The terrain is demanding with significant elevation change and technical descents. This is wilderness skiing at its most raw — no spectators, no shortcuts, no shelter.
course.surface: Groomed tracks through mountain wilderness; some sections wind-affected
course.grooming: Functional grooming in remote conditions; wind can affect track quality on exposed sections
features: Crosses Vindelfjällen Nature Reserve — one of Europe's largest
Follows ancient Sami reindeer migration routes
Exposed mountain plateaus and sheltered valleys
Among the most remote ski races in Scandinavia
Views of the Vindel Mountains and frozen mountain lakes

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Maximum scenery and snow scores for this wilderness mountain race. The extreme remoteness limits accessibility, field size, and organization scores. The course technicality is high with exposed mountain terrain. A bucket-list adventure race rather than a polished marathon event.

history.summary: Founded around 1985, Kungsledenrännet is an 80km wilderness classic race through the Vindelfjällen Nature Reserve in northern Sweden, above the Arctic Circle. The name references the Kungsleden ('King's Trail'), Sweden's most famous long-distance hiking route. The race follows ancient Sami migration paths through one of Europe's largest protected mountain areas. It remains a niche event, attracting adventurous skiers who seek genuine wilderness racing far from the groomed boulevards of mainstream marathons.
notable_facts: Crosses Vindelfjällen Nature Reserve — 5,600 km² of protected wilderness
Named after the Kungsleden (King's Trail), Sweden's iconic hiking route
Above the Arctic Circle at approximately 66°N latitude
Follows traditional Sami reindeer herding migration routes
One of the most remote competitive ski races in Europe
April date ensures snow while offering longer daylight hours
